description: You are a systems integrator deploying a digital signage solution for a flagship retail store. Create a VideoLAN Manager (.vlm) configuration file at /home/ga/Documents/signage.vlm to continuously broadcast three promo videos from /home/ga/Videos/promos/ to specific HTTP ports. Define three broadcast channels: 'window' (using window_promo.mp4), 'entrance' (using entrance_promo.mp4), and 'checkout' (using checkout_promo.mp4). Each channel must loop continuously and stream via HTTP multiplexed as MPEG-TS (mux=ts). The 'window' and 'checkout' streams must have audio disabled to save bandwidth, while the 'entrance' stream must retain both video and audio. Destinations are: window to http://127.0.0.1:8081/window, entrance to http://127.0.0.1:8082/entrance, and checkout to http://127.0.0.1:8083/checkout. Create and execute a bash script at /home/ga/start_signage.sh that launches VLC in headless mode (cvlc), loads the VLM configuration, and runs the VLM daemon in the background so that all streams are active upon completion.
